original_hypothesis.title: Landlord Tax Rate Bait-and-Switch Exploits Next-Period Rate Lock
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: updateTaxRate does not trigger farming for existing stakers before changing the rate. While latestTaxRate in toilerState protects the current period, the NEW rate is immediately stored for the NEXT period when any staker farms. A landlord can attract stakers with a low tax rate, wait for them to farm (locking the low rate for the current period), then immediately raise the rate to MAX. All subsequent farming periods use MAX tax rate. The renters must notice and unstake manually, losing time-value during the reaction window.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The tax rate update is instant with no cooldown or notice period. latestTaxRate provides single-period protection but the new rate takes effect on the very next farm call. Observable on-chain state (tax rate change event) is the only signal to renters, who may not monitor in real-time.

proof_of_signal.pattern: updateTaxRate directly sets plotMetadata[landlord].currentTaxRate without farming existing stakers; _farmPlots line 262 stores new rate as latestTaxRate for next period

preconditions: Landlord has stakers on their plots
Stakers recently farmed (so current period is protected by old latestTaxRate)
Landlord changes tax rate to MAX_TAX_RATE
Stakers do not immediately notice and unstake

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: toilerState[tokenId].latestTaxRate = plotMetadata[_toiler.landlord].currentTaxRate at line 262 — stores whatever the current rate is for the next period
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Landlord: set low rate → attract stakers → wait for them to farm → set MAX rate → earn MAX tax on all subsequent periods until stakers react
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Landlord extracts (MAX_TAX_RATE - initial_low_rate) * schnibbles_per_period * reaction_time_periods from unsuspecting stakers

economic_questions: What is the delta between MIN and MAX tax rates?
What is the expected reaction time for renters to detect and respond to rate changes?
How much value can a landlord extract during the reaction window?
validation_requirements: Determine MIN_TAX_RATE and MAX_TAX_RATE from deployed config
Calculate extractable value per period at MAX vs MIN rates
Assess monitoring infrastructure available to renters

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Locked value to create plots (landlord's own capital, not lost)
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Proportional to tax rate spread and staker reaction time
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Until stakers unstake; repeatable with new stakers

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: None beyond locked value for plots
market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Immediately after stakers farm to maximize next-period extraction
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Renter trust erosion; unfair value extraction

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All renters staked on the landlord's plots
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Loss of (MAX - advertised) rate schnibbles per period
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All schnibbles generated on landlord's plots during bait-switch window

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: MIN_TAX_RATE, MAX_TAX_RATE, typical staker farming frequency
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Model extraction over realistic reaction windows

validation_result.mechanism: Missing pre-change farming in updateTaxRate enables bait-and-switch
validation_result.impact_summary: Landlord can attract stakers with low tax, then instantly raise to MAX_TAX_RATE. All subsequent farming periods use the new MAX rate until stakers manually detect and unstake, extracting excess tax during the reaction window.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The hypothesis accurately describes a code defect where updateTaxRate directly sets plotMetadata[landlord].currentTaxRate without first farming existing stakers. The latestTaxRate snapshot in toilerState provides single-period protection (the currently unfarmed period uses the old rate), but when _farmPlots executes, it stores the NEW currentTaxRate into toilerState[tokenId].latestTaxRate at line ~262. This means every subsequent farming period immediately uses the new (potentially MAX) rate. The attack flow is: (1) landlord sets low rate to attract stakers, (2) stakers farm, locking the low rate for the current period, (3) landlord calls updateTaxRate(MAX_TAX_RATE), (4) stakers' next farm call uses the old latestTaxRate for the current elapsed period but stores MAX for all future periods, (5) stakers pay MAX tax until they notice and unstake. There is no cooldown, timelock, or notice mechanism to protect renters.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The defect is confirmed by tracing two code paths. First, updateTaxRate (lines 98-106) directly writes plotMetadata[landlord].currentTaxRate = newTaxRate with no call to _farmPlots for existing stakers. Second, _farmPlots uses _toiler.latestTaxRate (the old snapshot) for the current period's tax calculation, but then overwrites it with plotMetadata[_toiler.landlord].currentTaxRate (the new rate) for future periods. This creates a one-period grace window but allows immediate exploitation for all subsequent periods.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: updateTaxRate changes the tax rate immediately without farming existing stakers first. No cooldown or notice period. This enables bait-and-switch: set low rate to attract stakers, then raise to MAX immediately after they farm.

The current period uses _toiler.latestTaxRate (old snapshot, protected). But the code immediately stores the current plotMetadata rate (potentially changed to MAX by landlord) into latestTaxRate for the NEXT period. This means one period of protection, then all subsequent periods use the new rate.

calling_sequence: 1. Landlord calls updateTaxRate(MAX_TAX_RATE) - directly sets plotMetadata[landlord].currentTaxRate = MAX_TAX_RATE (line 105)
3. In _farmPlots loop: _toiler = toilerState[tokenId] loads OLD latestTaxRate (line 233)
4. schnibblesLandlord = (schnibblesTotal * _toiler.latestTaxRate) / 1e18 - uses OLD rate for current period (line 259)
6. On next farmPlots call, step 3 loads MAX_TAX_RATE as _toiler.latestTaxRate, applying MAX tax for all subsequent periods
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The vulnerability exists because updateTaxRate at line 105 directly assigns the new rate without iterating through stakers to farm them first. If updateTaxRate first called _farmPlots for all stakers on the landlord's plots, all accrued time would be settled at the old rate, and only truly future periods would use the new rate. Without this, the landlord can change the rate between any two consecutive farm calls by a staker, and the new rate takes effect for the very next period after the staker's next farm.

deployment_assumptions_checked: MIN_TAX_RATE and MAX_TAX_RATE provide meaningful range for bait-and-switch
config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment scripts configure MIN_TAX_RATE or MAX_TAX_RATE directly. The _reconfigure function loads them from config storage using potentially wrong StorageKeys (StorageKey.LockManager and StorageKey.AccountManager respectively), which is a separate issue. If properly configured, the spread between MIN and MAX determines the severity of the bait-and-switch.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: The attack scenario is valid: (1) Landlord creates plots by locking value. (2) Landlord sets currentTaxRate to MIN_TAX_RATE via updateTaxRate. (3) Renters are attracted by the low rate and stake NFTs via stakeMunchable, which snapshots the low rate into toilerState[tokenId].latestTaxRate. (4) Renters farm (or any action with forceFarmPlots modifier triggers farming), settling the current period at the low rate and storing the low rate again as latestTaxRate. (5) Landlord immediately calls updateTaxRate(MAX_TAX_RATE). plotMetadata[landlord].currentTaxRate = MAX_TAX_RATE. (6) When renters next farm, their current period is protected (uses old latestTaxRate), but line 262 stores MAX_TAX_RATE into latestTaxRate. (7) All subsequent farming periods use MAX_TAX_RATE until renters unstake. The attack is repeatable with new stakers.
validation_result.impact_assessment: The impact is proportional to (MAX_TAX_RATE - MIN_TAX_RATE) * BASE_SCHNIBBLE_RATE * reaction_time * number_of_stakers. Stakers lose schnibbles to the landlord's excess tax during the window between rate change and their unstaking. Since schnibbles represent in-protocol value (used for player progression), this constitutes unfair value extraction. The attack is low-cost (single updateTaxRate gas call) and repeatable. The severity depends on actual MIN/MAX config values and how quickly stakers monitor for TaxRateChanged events.
recommended_mitigations: Add a mechanism in updateTaxRate that farms all stakers on the landlord's plots before changing the rate, ensuring accrued time is settled at the old rate
Implement a timelock or notice period for tax rate changes (e.g., rate changes take effect after N blocks/hours)
Cap the maximum rate increase per time period to prevent sudden jumps from MIN to MAX
Allow stakers to set a maximum acceptable tax rate; if the landlord exceeds it, auto-dirty the staker's position so they can unstake without penalty
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy LandManager with MIN_TAX_RATE=1e16 (1%) and MAX_TAX_RATE=5e17 (50%). 2. Landlord locks value to create plots, sets tax to 1%. 3. Renter stakes NFT on landlord's plot. 4. Advance time 1 day. Renter calls farmPlots() - verify low tax applied. 5. Landlord calls updateTaxRate(5e17). 6. Advance time 1 day. Renter calls farmPlots() - current period uses 1% (protected), but latestTaxRate now stores 50%. 7. Advance time 1 day. Renter calls farmPlots() - this period uses 50%, confirming the bait-and-switch.
uncertainties: Actual MIN_TAX_RATE and MAX_TAX_RATE values are loaded via potentially mismatched StorageKeys (separate bug in _reconfigure), making the real spread uncertain
The economic impact depends on schnibbles value, which is an internal game currency with unclear external value
Renter monitoring infrastructure (event watchers, dashboards) may reduce the effective reaction window
additional_testing_needed: Verify actual deployed MIN_TAX_RATE and MAX_TAX_RATE values to quantify the maximum tax spread
Test the exact timing: can landlord change rate in the same block after a staker farms?
Measure typical staker farming frequency to estimate realistic reaction windows
Test interaction with the forceFarmPlots modifier - does staking/unstaking/transferring also lock in the new rate?
